title: Indicator (long-form fact table)
description: A single socio-economic, demographic, infrastructure, or fiscal indicator stored as long-form rows: (entity_kind, entity_id, time, value, unit, ...). Joined to geometry at view time via the existing identifier conventions (ECI state codes, LGD district codes). Indicators carry semantic hints (`value_kind`, `direction`) so the frontend's generic chart components can render them without per-indicator code.

x-changelog.description: Initial schema. Long-form indicator with declared time_grain, value_kind, direction, unit. Created for the socio-economic expansion (TODO/SOCIO-ECONOMIC-EXPANSION.md, decisions D1/D4/Q3, locked 2026-05-10).

sources.description: Provenance per CLAUDE.md §12 / ADR-0002, extended per D3 with optional name + authority for human attribution.

indicator.description: Identity and rendering metadata for the indicator. The four hint fields (value_kind, direction, scale_hint, unit) drive the generic chart components — every indicator is renderable without per-indicator UI code (D1, design-system principle).

id.description: Stable namespaced id (e.g. "energy/installed_mw_by_state", "economy/gsdp_constant_prices_2011_12"). Used for cross-references and URL slugs.

title.description: Human-readable title shown in legends and headings.

description.description: One-paragraph definition. Citizen-readable — avoid jargon, or translate it.

entity_kind.description: What the indicator is measured per. Joins to the matching identifier convention (ECI state, LGD district, etc.).

time_grain.description: Resolution of the `time` field on each row. `year`/`fiscal_year` → YYYY; `quarter`/`month` → YYYY-MM; `date` → YYYY-MM-DD. Per Q3 (TODO/SOCIO-ECONOMIC-EXPANSION.md).

value_kind.description: Drives formatting and choice of scale. `count` → integer; `rate` → per-unit (e.g. per capita); `share` → 0..1; `currency` → with `unit`; `index` → unitless ratio; `duration` → with `unit` (days, hours); `raw` → no formatter (escape hatch).

direction.description: Drives the colour ramp choice. `higher_is_better` → sequential warm; `lower_is_better` → sequential warm reversed; `neutral` → sequential cool. Diverging ramps are reserved for explicit comparison views and are not chosen automatically.

scale_hint.description: Suggested d3 scale type; the chart may override if the data demands.

unit.description: Unit string (e.g. "MW", "INR (crore)", "per 100k", "%", "years"). For `share`, use "%" or "share" to disambiguate display.

denominator.description: When this indicator is itself a derived per-X rate, the indicator id of the denominator (e.g. "people/population_total"). Null for raw indicators.

notes.description: Caveats the citizen should know (base year, methodology change, sample frame). Surfaced in the chart's source card.

rows.description: Long-form fact rows. Every row carries the entity id (matching `indicator.entity_kind`), a time stamp at the declared `time_grain`, and a numeric value.

entity_id.description: Identifier matching `indicator.entity_kind` (e.g. "S22" for entity_kind=state, "S22-167" for constituency).

time.description: YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD. Must be consistent with `indicator.time_grain` (validator-side check, not a JSON Schema constraint).

value.description: Numeric measurement. `null` is allowed and means "not available for this entity-time" (distinct from zero).

facet.description: Optional sub-category for stacked/grouped views (e.g. fuel type for installed capacity: "coal" / "hydro" / "solar"). Null when not faceted.
